title: AUC-safe foldwise sigmoid stacker instead of isotonic post-calibration
summary: The evaluation metric is ROC AUC.
rationale: The evaluation metric is ROC AUC. Scikit-learn explicitly notes that isotonic calibration can change AUC by introducing ties, while sigmoid calibration is strictly monotonic and preserves within-model ranking. That makes a foldwise sigmoid stacker a safer ensemble-finishing step than generic isotonic calibration.
implementation_hint: Collect OOF predictions from diverse base models or experts, then fit a logistic meta-model or `CalibratedClassifierCV(method='sigmoid')` on held-out folds only. Compare against plain rank averaging and avoid isotonic unless a leaderboard test justifies the extra tie risk.
expected_effect: Can stabilize cross-model probability scales and improve blend robustness without sacrificing the core ranking signal each base learner found.
risk: If base learners are highly correlated, the stacker adds little; averaging calibrated models can still move global rank slightly, so it needs direct AUC validation.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
